Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student in the school's reward system, holding their current points balance and activity history.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

name.description: The full name of the student.

nfcId.description: The NFC card ID associated with the student for quick login. This must be unique across all students.

points.description: The current points balance accumulated by the student.

teacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher who is assigned to this student. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Student)

history.description: A chronological log of all points earned or spent by the student.

Teacher.title: Teacher
Teacher.description: Represents a teacher who can generate coupons and manage assigned students.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Teacher entity.

name.description: The full name of the teacher.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines a classification for coupon types, such as 'Good Behavior' or 'Academic Achievement'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The descriptive name of the category (e.g., 'Good Behavior').

Coupon.title: Coupon
Coupon.description: Represents a digital coupon that students can redeem for points. Each coupon has a unique code and value.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Coupon entity, typically its barcode code.

value.description: The point value awarded when this coupon is redeemed.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this coupon belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Coupon)

teacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher who issued this coupon. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Coupon)

used.description: Indicates whether the coupon has been redeemed by a student.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the coupon was generated.

redeemedByStudentId.description: Optional reference to the Student who redeemed this coupon. Only present if 'used' is true. (Relationship: Student 1:N Coupon)

redeemedAt.description: Optional timestamp when the coupon was redeemed. Only present if 'used' is true.

RewardItem.title: RewardItem
RewardItem.description: Represents an item available for purchase in the student rewards shop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RewardItem entity.

name.description: The display name of the reward item (e.g., 'Candy Bar').

cost.description: The number of points required for a student to redeem this reward.

description.description: A brief description of the reward item.

iconName.description: The name of a Lucide icon associated with the reward item for UI display.

StudentActivity.title: StudentActivity
StudentActivity.description: Represents a single entry in a student's activity history, detailing point changes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentActivity entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student who performed this activity. (Relationship: Student 1:N StudentActivity)

description.description: A textual description of the activity (e.g., 'Redeemed coupon', 'Bought Candy Bar').

amount.description: The change in points for this activity (+ for earned, - for spent).

date.description: Timestamp of when the activity occurred.

couponId.description: Optional reference to the Coupon redeemed, if applicable. (Relationship: Coupon 1:N StudentActivity)

rewardItemId.description: Optional reference to the RewardItem purchased, if applicable. (Relationship: RewardItem 1:N StudentActivity)

definition.description: The root collection for all data associated with a specific school. Document ID is the school's unique identifier. The document contains metadata about the school, acting as a security boundary.

Student profiles. Each document includes denormalized 'authUid' (Firebase Auth UID) for direct student login and 'teacherId' for assignment to a teacher, ensuring authorization independence. The 'history' array from the schema is conceptually moved to a subcollection for scalability.

Detailed chronological log of point changes for a specific student. Documents include denormalized 'studentId' and 'teacherId' (of the assigned teacher) for robust authorization independence, allowing teachers to query their students' activities directly (QAPs).
Teacher profiles. Each document includes denormalized 'authUid' (Firebase Auth UID) for direct teacher login and authorization checks.
Definitions for coupon categories, managed by administrators for selection in coupon generation.
Details of generated coupons, identified by a unique barcode/ID. Includes 'teacherId' (issuer) and 'redeemedByStudentId' (if used) for authorization independence and tracking.
Items available for purchase in the student rewards shop, managed by administrators and publicly readable by students.
Used for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at `{adminUid}` path grants full admin privileges for that specific school to the corresponding Firebase Auth UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the 'Arcade Rewards Hub' is designed to be multi-tenant, with all data logically isolated per school under a top-level `/schools/{schoolId}` collection. This ensures that each school's data is entirely separate and access is scoped to authenticated users within that school.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design achieves Authorization Independence by denormalizing key authorization fields directly into relevant documents, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules. Specifically:

1.  **Student Documents (`/schools/{schoolId}/students/{studentId}`):** Each student document will contain an `authUid` field, which stores the Firebase Authentication User ID of the logged-in student. This allows a student to directly access their own student profile using `request.auth.uid == resource.data.authUid` without needing to perform a `get()` on a separate user profile document.
2.  **Teacher Documents (`/schools/{schoolId}/teachers/{teacherId}`):** Similarly, each teacher document will include an `authUid` field (where `teacherId` is ideally the `authUid` itself) for direct authentication and rule evaluation using `request.auth.uid == resource.data.authUid`.
3.  **StudentActivity Documents (`/schools/{schoolId}/students/{studentId}/activities/{activityId}`):** To enable teachers to efficiently list the activity history of *their* students, each `StudentActivity` document will denormalize the `teacherId` of the student's assigned teacher. This means a teacher can query `activities` documents where `resource.data.teacherId == request.auth.uid` (assuming `teacherId` is the teacher's `authUid`) without performing a `get()` on the parent `student` document.
4.  **Coupon Documents (`/schools/{schoolId}/coupons/{couponId}`):** These already include `teacherId` (the issuer's ID) and `redeemedByStudentId` (if used). This denormalization supports teachers listing their own generated coupons and students being able to verify/redeem specific coupons atomically.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
This structure directly supports QAPs by ensuring that `list` operations can be secured with conditions that match the query. For instance:

*   **Student Self-Access:** A student can query their own activities at `/schools/{schoolId}/students/{request.auth.uid}/activities` where the `studentId` wildcard implicitly matches their UID, and further secured by `resource.data.studentId == request.auth.uid` or the path directly containing their UID.
*   **Teacher Student Management:** A teacher can query for *their* students by using a query like `collection('students').where('teacherId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The security rule for `/schools/{schoolId}/students` can then allow `list` if `resource.data.teacherId == request.auth.uid` (assuming the teacher's document ID or `authUid` is stored in `teacherId`), making it QAP-compliant.
*   **Teacher Activity View:** A teacher can list activities for all their students by querying `/schools/{schoolId}/students/{studentId}/activities` where `resource.data.teacherId == request.auth.uid`. The denormalized `teacherId` in each activity document makes this a direct and efficient query that can be secured with a `list` rule.
*   **Global Readable Data:** Collections like `categories` and `rewardItems` are designed for broad read access (e.g., by any student or teacher for dropdowns/shop display) but restricted write access (only admins), making `list` operations inherently secure and QAP-compliant by allowing universal reads.

This robust denormalization strategy, coupled with clear path segregation and the `schools/{schoolId}` root, ensures that security rules are simple, efficient, atomic, and debuggable, adhering to all core design principles.
